title_en: 5. Would you like to meet him outside the institution? (cont.)
content_en: Meanwhile, ‘Umar’s wife called out from inside the tent and said, “O Amir al-Mu’minin! Glad tidings of a baby boy!” When the man heard the words, “Amir al-Mu’minin” he became shocked and said, “Are you the Caliph, ‘Umar bin al-Khattab?” “Yes, I am”, replied ‘Umar. The man was bemused and began to distance himself from ‘Umar. ‘Umar said, “Stay in your place.” To worship in secret He then got hold of the plate, placed it next to the tent and called out to his wife, Umm Kulthum saying, “Take some food for her.” The woman then ate some food and left the rest outside the tent. ‘Umar then took the plate and placed it in front of the man and said, “Have something to eat, for you haven’t slept since last night.” ‘Umar then called his wife out of the tent and said to the man, “Do come to us tomorrow and we will give you whatever you wish.” May Allah have mercy on ‘Umar for his humbleness and secret acts of worship which he did for the sole objective of earning Allah’s love. ‘Ali bin al-Husayn – may Allah have mercy upon him and his father – would carry a sack of bread on his back at night and give it in charity. He would say, “Giving charity in secret extinguishes Allah’s wrath.” When he died, black marks were found on his back, and it was said, “This is the back of a person who carries loads, but as far as we are aware, he never worked as someone who did that.” When the food supply stopped reaching a hundred homes in Madinah - and these were the homes of widows and orphans who would receive their food supply at night while not knowing who delivered it to them – people realised that it was ‘Ali bin al-Husayn who would bring these food supplies and spend on these people. One of the Salaf fasted for twenty years, fasting a day and not fasting the next, whilst his family had absolutely no knowledge of it. He would have a shop that he would go to at sunrise taking To worship in secret with him his breakfast and lunch. If it was the day he was fasting, he would give his food in charity. If it was the day he was not fasting, he would eat the food. After sunset, he would return to his family and have dinner with them. Yes! They would endeavour to make their acts of worship solely for Allah. They are the ones who are truly pious and about whom Allah says, “Indeed! For the duteous is achievement - Gardens enclosed and vineyards, and full-breasted maidens of companions; And a full cup. There hear they never vain discourse, nor lying − Requital from thy Lord − a gift in payment.” Seek the love of the Creator, and He will place in the hearts of the creation the love for you. Enlightenment… The objective is not to make people love you superﬁcially, but they should really love you in their hearts. To worship in secret (66) Take them out of the ditch Has it ever happened to you that a person humiliated you publicly by insulting or ridiculing you about anything, even if it be about something as minor as your clothes, your words or your manners, such that your face changed colour as your discomfort became visible? But then, suddenly, another person came to your defence and you felt greatly indebted to him for helping you? Perhaps it was as if he had grabbed hold of your clothes to rescue you just as someone else tried to push you into a ditch. Practise this skill with others and you will see it having wondrous effects. You visit a person and notice his son coming with a plate of food, and while in the process of doing so, he hurries and nearly drops it. His father becomes angry at him and screams, “Why the hurry? How many times must I tell you not to hurry?” You notice that the son’s face turns red with embarrassment. You say, “No. Don’t say that. He’s the man! MashaAllah, look at him, he is carrying all of this by himself.
title_id: 5. Maukah Kamu Menemuinya di Luar Lembaga? (lanjutan)
content_id: Sementara itu, istri 'Umar berseru dari dalam tenda dan berkata, "Wahai Amir al-Mu'minin! Berita gembira tentang seorang bayi laki-laki!" Ketika pria itu mendengar kata-kata, "Amir al-Mu'minin", ia terkejut dan berkata, "Apakah kamu adalah Khalifah, 'Umar bin al-Khaththab?" "Ya, akulah", jawab 'Umar. Pria itu bingung dan mulai menjauh dari 'Umar. 'Umar berkata, "Tetaplah di tempatmu." Ia kemudian mengambil piring, meletakkannya di dekat tenda, dan memanggil istrinya, Umm Kultsum, berkata, "Bawakan sedikit makanan untuknya." Wanita itu kemudian memakan sedikit makanan dan menyisakan sisanya di luar tenda. 'Umar kemudian mengambil piring dan meletakkannya di depan pria itu dan berkata, "Makanlah, karena kamu belum tidur sejak semalam." 'Umar kemudian memanggil istrinya keluar dari tenda dan berkata kepada pria itu, "Datanglah kepada kami besok dan kami akan memberimu apa yang kamu inginkan." Semoga Allah merahmati 'Umar atas kerendahan hati dan ibadah-ibadah rahasia yang ia lakukan semata-mata demi mendapatkan cinta Allah. 'Ali bin al-Husayn — semoga Allah merahmatinya dan ayahnya — biasa membawa sekarung roti di punggungnya pada malam hari dan memberikannya sebagai sedekah. Ia biasa berkata, "Bersedekah secara diam-diam memadamkan kemurkaan Allah." Ketika ia meninggal, ditemukan bekas hitam di punggungnya, dan dikatakan, "Ini adalah punggung seseorang yang biasa membawa beban, tetapi sejauh yang kami ketahui, ia tidak pernah bekerja sebagai seseorang yang melakukan itu." Ketika pasokan makanan berhenti mencapai seratus rumah di Madinah — dan ini adalah rumah-rumah para janda dan yatim piatu yang biasa menerima pasokan makanan mereka di malam hari tanpa mengetahui siapa yang mengirimkannya — orang-orang menyadari bahwa 'Ali bin al-Husayn-lah yang biasa membawa pasokan makanan ini dan menafkahi orang-orang ini. Salah seorang ulama Salaf berpuasa selama dua puluh tahun, berpuasa sehari dan tidak berpuasa hari berikutnya, sementara keluarganya sama sekali tidak mengetahuinya. Ia memiliki sebuah toko yang ia datangi saat matahari terbit membawa sarapan dan makan siangnya. Jika itu adalah hari puasanya, ia memberikan makanannya sebagai sedekah. Jika itu adalah hari tidak puasanya, ia memakan makanannya. Setelah matahari terbenam, ia kembali ke keluarganya dan makan malam bersama mereka. Ya! Mereka berjuang keras untuk membuat ibadah-ibadah mereka semata-mata karena Allah. Merekalah orang-orang yang benar-benar bertakwa dan tentang merekalah Allah berfirman, "Sesungguhnya bagi orang-orang yang bertakwa terdapat keberuntungan — Kebun-kebun dan anggur, dan perempuan-perempuan muda yang montok sebagai kawan, dan piala-piala yang penuh. Di sana mereka tidak mendengar ucapan yang sia-sia dan tidak (pula) perkataan dusta — Balasan dari Tuhanmu, karunia yang cukup." Carilah cinta Sang Pencipta, dan Dia akan menempatkan dalam hati ciptaan cinta kepada-Mu. Pencerahan... Tujuannya bukan agar orang mencintaimu secara lahiriah, tetapi mereka harus benar-benar mencintaimu dari dalam hati mereka. (66) Keluarkan Mereka dari Lubang: Pernahkah terjadi padamu bahwa seseorang mempermalukanmu di depan umum dengan menghina atau mengejekmu tentang apapun, meskipun itu tentang hal-hal sekecil pakaianmu, perkataanmu, atau sikapmu, sehingga wajahmu berubah warna karena ketidaknyamananmu terlihat? Tetapi kemudian, tiba-tiba, orang lain datang membelamu dan kamu merasa sangat berhutang budi kepadanya karena telah membantumu? Mungkin seperti seolah-olah ia telah memegang bajumu untuk menyelamatkanmu tepat ketika orang lain mencoba mendorongmu ke dalam sebuah lubang. Praktikkan keterampilan ini dengan orang lain dan kamu akan melihatnya memiliki efek yang menakjubkan. Kamu mengunjungi seseorang dan memperhatikan anaknya datang membawa sepiring makanan, dan saat dalam prosesnya, ia terburu-buru dan hampir menjatuhkannya. Ayahnya marah padanya dan berteriak, "Mengapa terburu-buru? Berapa kali harus aku katakan padamu untuk tidak terburu-buru?" Kamu memperhatikan bahwa wajah anak itu memerah karena malu. Kamu berkata, "Tidak. Jangan bilang begitu. Dia hebat! MasyaAllah, lihatlah dia, ia membawa semua ini sendirian.
